task_completion.description: Jolly Lobby Track application is launched and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of Jolly Lobby Track is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The database configuration or connection settings dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for 'Database Setup', 'Connection Settings', 'Properties', or similar is open within Jolly Lobby Track.

task_completion.description: The correct archived database path is entered into the configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/Documents/visitor_archive_2024.mdb' (or its Wine equivalent, e.g., 'Z:\home\ga\Documents\visitor_archive_2024.mdb') is visible in the database file selection or connection string text field.

task_completion.description: The application successfully connects to the new database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Connection Successful' message, the configuration dialog closing without errors, or the main window updating to reflect the new database path/records.

task_completion.description: The application remains open at the end of the task.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the final screenshot of the trajectory, the Jolly Lobby Track application window is still open and active.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's GUI to configure the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Jolly Lobby Track menus and dialogs rather than just using terminal commands to edit registry keys or config files.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Jolly Lobby Track software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application shown is the genuine Jolly Lobby Track interface running via Wine, not a text editor or a mocked-up window.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external scripts to bypass the manual configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing the execution of Python or Bash scripts designed to directly modify the Wine registry ('HKCU\Software\Jolly Technologies') or create lock files artificially.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target database file is '/home/ga/Documents/visitor_archive_2024.mdb'. Because Jolly Lobby Track runs via Wine, the agent might enter or the application might display the Windows-mapped equivalent path (e.g., 'Z:\home\ga\Documents\visitor_archive_2024.mdb'). A successful connection typically generates a lock file (.ldb, .laccdb, or .slock) in the same directory, but visual confirmation of the path in the app's connection settings is the primary indicator of success. The application must remain open in the final state.
